config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diutamakan tanpa kontrol, mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat dan hak dasar tiap orang. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan yang jelas: masyarakat kuat justru ketika individu tidak dikorbankan seenaknya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, I am wary of any statement that gives “society” too much power, because that can quickly become a justification for trampling minorities, unpopular speech, or basic civil liberties in the name of the greater good. At the same time, individual rights are not a license to harm others, so there are real limits when public safety or the rights of other people are at stake. To me, the right balance is that society can set narrow rules for genuine common needs, but individual rights should remain the default priority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, istilah "demi kepentingan bersama" terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, menutup kritik, atau mengorbankan kelompok yang lemah, padahal justru di situlah hak individu paling perlu dilindungi. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi menurut saya harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dilanggar sembarangan. Kalau tidak, yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang lebih baik, melainkan kekuasaan yang lebih bebas atas individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should never be treated as a blanket priority over individual rights. From a United States perspective, I am very skeptical of any argument that gives “society” the first claim, because history shows how easily that language can be used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. Public safety and shared rules matter, but they should be limited and specific, not a free pass to override basic liberties whenever it is convenient. To me, a healthy society protects individuals first and asks people to accept limits only when there is a clear, necessary, and narrowly defined public reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering jadi pembenaran untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau mengorbankan orang kecil yang tidak punya kuasa tawar. Menurut saya, masyarakat justru sehat kalau hak dasar individu dijadikan batas yang jelas, lalu kepentingan bersama dicapai lewat aturan yang adil dan proporsional. Kepentingan sosial penting, tetapi tidak boleh diberi cek kosong untuk mengalahkan martabat manusia.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my American perspective, I do not trust broad claims about "the common good" because they are often used to justify censorship, discrimination, or government overreach against unpopular people. That does not mean individual rights are absolute in every situation, since public safety and the rights of others can require real limits, but those limits should be narrow and carefully justified. In my view, the burden should be on society to prove why a specific restriction is necessary, not on individuals to surrender their rights by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
